goto-meeting.name: GoTo Meeting
goto-meeting.short_description: GoTo Meeting is a longstanding video conferencing platform with HD video, screen sharing, cloud recording, and AI meeting transcription.
goto-meeting.starting_price: From $12 per organizer/month, billed annually (Professional plan)

features.description: Host meetings with high-definition video and clear audio for participants joining from a browser, desktop app, or mobile device.
features.title: HD video and audio conferencing

features.description: Share a full desktop, single application window, or browser tab so participants can follow a presentation, demo, or document review.
features.title: Screen sharing

features.description: Automatically records meetings, generates transcripts, and produces AI-written summaries that highlight key topics and action items.
features.title: Smart Meeting Assistant (AI transcription and summaries)

features.description: Record sessions to the cloud so they can be reviewed, shared, or transcribed after the meeting ends without local storage management.
features.title: Cloud recording

features.description: Mark up a shared screen in real time with pointers, shapes, and freehand drawing to guide participants through a walkthrough.
features.title: Drawing and annotation tools

features.description: Every organizer gets a fixed, reusable meeting link and dial-in number, so recurring meetings don't need a new invite each time.
features.title: Personal meeting room

features.description: Company admins can manage organizer licenses, lock meetings, control entry, and review account-wide usage from a central dashboard.
features.title: Admin and security controls

features.description: Native apps for Windows, Mac, iOS, and Android let hosts and participants start or join meetings without relying on a browser.
features.title: Mobile and desktop apps

features.description: Schedule and launch meetings directly from Outlook or Google Calendar without switching between separate scheduling tools.
features.title: Calendar integrations

pros: One of the most established, reliable video conferencing products, with a long track record for call stability
Smart Meeting Assistant provides useful AI transcripts and summaries without needing a separate note-taking tool
Simple, low-friction joining experience for guests, including browser-based join and phone dial-in options
Fits naturally into the broader GoTo Connect suite for businesses already using GoTo for phone or IT support
Centralized admin controls make it easier to manage licenses and security across larger organizer counts
cons: No permanent free plan, only a time-limited free trial, unlike several competing video conferencing tools
Entry-level Professional plan caps participants at 50, which is limiting for larger team meetings
Not designed for large public webinars or virtual events, which require GoTo's separate webinar product
Interface feels dated next to newer collaboration-first tools with deeper chat and whiteboard features
Less tightly integrated into Microsoft Teams or Google Workspace than each platform's own native meeting tool

faqs.answer: No. GoTo Meeting does not offer a permanent free tier; it offers a 14-day free trial of its paid plans, after which a Professional, Business, or Enterprise subscription is required to keep hosting meetings.
faqs.question: Does GoTo Meeting have a free plan?

faqs.answer: Capacity depends on the plan: the Professional plan supports up to 50 participants, the Business plan up to 250, and the Enterprise plan up to 3,000 participants per meeting.
faqs.question: How many people can join a GoTo Meeting session?

faqs.answer: Yes. Its Smart Meeting Assistant automatically records and transcribes meetings and generates an AI summary highlighting key discussion points and action items, included with Business and Enterprise plans.
faqs.question: Does GoTo Meeting include AI transcription and meeting summaries?

faqs.answer: No. GoTo Meeting is designed for standard meetings and client calls, while GoTo Webinar is a separate product built for large-scale webinars with registration pages and audience marketing tools.
faqs.question: Is GoTo Meeting the same product as GoTo Webinar?

faqs.answer: GoTo Meeting is available through a web browser and has dedicated desktop apps for Windows and Mac, plus mobile apps for iOS and Android.
faqs.question: What platforms does GoTo Meeting run on?

features: Smart Meeting Assistant (AI transcripts and summaries)

dropbox-sign.name: Dropbox Sign
dropbox-sign.short_description: Dropbox Sign (formerly HelloSign) is an e-signature platform for sending, signing, and tracking legally binding documents online.

features.description: Upload a document, place signature, initial, date, and text fields, assign them to specific signers, and send the request by email for completion in a browser or mobile device.
features.title: Signature requests

features.description: Save frequently sent documents such as NDAs or standard contracts as templates so fields only need to be mapped once and can be reused for future signers.
features.title: Reusable templates

features.description: A developer API for embedding signature requests, templates, and embedded signing directly into a company's own application or workflow, with webhook support for status updates.
features.title: Dropbox Sign API

features.description: Every completed document includes a tamper-evident audit trail with signer identity details, timestamps, and IP information, plus a certificate of completion.
features.title: Audit trail and certificate of completion

features.description: Collect a signature from someone in the same physical location by handing them a shared device rather than sending an email request.
features.title: In-person signing

features.description: Send the same document to a list of recipients at once, each receiving their own individual copy to sign, useful for onboarding forms or standard agreements.
features.title: Bulk send

features.description: Add a company logo and adjust colors on the signing page so the request looks consistent with a business's own brand, available on Standard and Premium plans.
features.title: Custom branding

features.description: Connects with Dropbox, Google Workspace, Slack, HubSpot, Salesforce, and other business tools to trigger or store signature requests from existing workflows.
features.title: Integrations

features.description: Native iOS and Android apps let senders and signers prepare, send, and complete signature requests from a phone or tablet.
features.title: Mobile apps

pros: Clean, simple interface that most users can pick up without training
Free plan available for people who only need to sign occasionally
Well-documented, developer-friendly API for embedding signatures into other products
Tight integration with Dropbox for teams already storing files there
Generally competitively priced against larger e-signature suites like DocuSign
cons: Free plan is capped at 3 signature requests per month
Standard plan requires a minimum of 2 paid users
Premium plan pricing is not published and requires contacting sales
Fewer advanced contract lifecycle and document-generation features than tools like PandaDoc or DocuSign CLM
Custom branding, bulk send, and deeper integrations are only available on higher-tier plans

faqs.answer: Yes. Dropbox Sign is the rebranded name for HelloSign, an e-signature company founded in 2010 that Dropbox acquired in 2019. Dropbox renamed the product from HelloSign to Dropbox Sign in 2023; the underlying service and account history carried over.
faqs.question: Is Dropbox Sign the same as HelloSign?

faqs.answer: Dropbox Sign offers a free plan that allows up to 3 signature requests per month, with unlimited self-signing. Higher-volume or team use requires upgrading to a paid Essentials, Standard, or Premium plan.
faqs.question: Is Dropbox Sign free to use?

faqs.answer: Yes. The Dropbox Sign API lets developers embed signature requests, templates, and embedded signing directly into their own applications, with webhook support for tracking document status. API access is priced and packaged separately from the standard web app plans.
faqs.question: Does Dropbox Sign have an API?

faqs.answer: Dropbox Sign is designed to produce legally binding electronic signatures under laws such as the U.S. ESIGN Act and UETA, and each completed document includes an audit trail and certificate of completion recording signer details and timestamps. Specific legal requirements can vary by jurisdiction and document type.
faqs.question: Are documents signed with Dropbox Sign legally binding?

faqs.answer: Signers do not need any account to open and sign a document sent through Dropbox Sign. Senders sign up for a separate Dropbox Sign account, which can optionally be connected to an existing Dropbox account for storing and sending files.
faqs.question: Do I need a Dropbox account to use Dropbox Sign?

features: Up to 3 signature requests per month

qwilr.name: Qwilr
qwilr.short_description: Qwilr helps sales and agency teams turn proposals, quotes, and contracts into interactive web pages with e-signatures and payments.

features.description: A block-based editor lets teams assemble proposals from text, images, embedded video, pricing tables, and calculators, publishing the result as a web page instead of a file.
features.title: Interactive page builder

features.description: A library of reusable proposal and quote templates helps teams standardize layout and branding while speeding up document creation.
features.title: Templates

features.description: Buyers can review and sign a proposal or contract directly on the page, without downloading a separate document or third-party tool.
features.title: E-signature

features.description: Payment collection is built into the page itself, so a buyer can accept a proposal and pay in the same flow.
features.title: QwilrPay

features.description: Per-page and per-section tracking shows how long a recipient viewed each part of a document, when it was reopened, and whether it was shared internally.
features.title: Engagement analytics

features.description: Native connections to HubSpot, Salesforce, Zoho, and Pipedrive let proposals be generated and tracked without leaving the CRM record.
features.title: CRM integrations

features.description: Higher-tier plans allow blocks or sections to show or hide based on choices the buyer makes elsewhere in the page.
features.title: Conditional content

features.description: An AI-assisted drafting feature on the Scale plan helps prefill proposal content to speed up document creation.
features.title: Smart Proposal Engine (AI Prefill)

features.description: An API is available on every plan for teams that want to build custom workflows or connect Qwilr to other internal systems.
features.title: API access

features.description: Growth and Scale plans add custom fonts and branding, identity verification, and team-level permissions for larger organizations.
features.title: Custom branding and permissions

pros: Turns proposals and quotes into interactive, on-brand web pages instead of static files
Combines e-signature and payment collection in a single buyer-facing flow
Detailed engagement analytics show exactly how recipients interact with a document
Native integrations with major CRMs like HubSpot and Salesforce
14-day free trial with no credit card required to test the editor
cons: No free plan, and pricing is per seat rather than per document
Growth and Scale tiers require five and ten seats minimum, raising the entry cost for small teams
Interface and pages are English-first and do not support right-to-left languages
Advanced features like conditional content and the Smart Proposal Engine are reserved for higher tiers
Not designed for heavy configure-price-quote logic or complex multi-party contract redlining

faqs.answer: Qwilr is used to create proposals, quotes, and sales contracts as interactive web pages that buyers can view, sign, and pay for online instead of receiving a static PDF or Word document.
faqs.question: What is Qwilr used for?

faqs.answer: No, Qwilr does not offer a permanent free plan. It offers a 14-day free trial on the Starter tier that does not require a credit card, after which a paid subscription is required.
faqs.question: Does Qwilr have a free plan?

faqs.answer: Qwilr's Starter plan starts at $35 per user per month when billed annually, with Growth and Scale tiers priced higher and requiring five and ten user seats minimum respectively.
faqs.question: How much does Qwilr cost?

faqs.answer: Yes. Qwilr includes built-in e-signature so buyers can accept documents on the page, and QwilrPay allows payment collection at the point of acceptance.
faqs.question: Does Qwilr support e-signatures and payments?

faqs.answer: Qwilr integrates with HubSpot, Salesforce, Zoho, Pipedrive, Quickbooks, Stripe, Slack, and Zapier, and also offers an API for custom integrations.
faqs.question: Which tools does Qwilr integrate with?

pricing_plans.billing_cycle: Annual (monthly billing also available at a higher rate)

mirotalk.name: MiroTalk
mirotalk.short_description: Self-hosted, open-source WebRTC video conferencing platform with peer-to-peer, SFU, and end-to-end encrypted call modes.

features.description: MiroTalk P2P connects participants directly over WebRTC without routing media through a central server.
features.title: Peer-to-peer video calls (P2P)

features.description: MiroTalk SFU uses a selective forwarding unit architecture to support more participants than pure P2P allows.
features.title: SFU mode for larger meetings

features.description: Supports video up to 8K resolution at 60fps along with screen sharing and recording.
features.title: High-resolution video

features.description: Includes text chat with markdown support and a collaborative whiteboard alongside video.

features.description: Can be deployed on your own server via Docker or Node.js in minutes.

pros: Free, open-source, and self-hostable under AGPLv3
No download, plug-in, or login required to join a call
Multiple architecture variants (P2P, SFU, C2C) to fit different use cases
Full REST API for embedding video calling into other products
Supports up to 8K/60fps video and multi-language interfaces
cons: The root mirotalk.com domain does not resolve to an active marketing site, which can make discovery confusing
Peer-to-peer mode's quality depends on client hardware and network conditions rather than server infrastructure
AGPLv3 requires derivative works to also be open source unless a separate commercial license is purchased
No official hosted enterprise support plan is documented

faqs.answer: Yes, MiroTalk is free and open source under the AGPLv3 license, and can be self-hosted at no licensing cost.
faqs.question: Is MiroTalk free to use?

faqs.answer: No, MiroTalk is designed so users can join calls without downloads, plug-ins, or logins.

faqs.answer: MiroTalk P2P connects participants directly to each other, while MiroTalk SFU routes media through a selective forwarding unit server to support larger meetings.
faqs.question: What's the difference between MiroTalk P2P and SFU?

faqs.answer: Yes, MiroTalk can be deployed on your own server using Docker or Node.js.
faqs.question: Can I self-host MiroTalk?

faqs.answer: Yes, in addition to the free AGPLv3 license, commercial licenses are available via CodeCanyon for closed-source use.

Official iOS and Android apps in addition to web access
Interoperates with other ActivityPub fediverse platforms
cons: No official managed hosting sold directly by the Mastodon project
Self-hosting an instance requires server administration skills
Sustained by donations and sponsors rather than a dedicated commercial support tier

faqs.answer: Yes, Mastodon is entirely free and open-source software, sustained by donations rather than paid tiers.
faqs.question: Is Mastodon free?

faqs.answer: Yes, anyone can self-host their own Mastodon instance, or join an existing public server such as mastodon.social.
faqs.question: Can I self-host Mastodon?

faqs.answer: Mastodon's source code is released under the AGPL-3.0 license.
faqs.question: What license is Mastodon released under?

faqs.answer: Yes, official iOS and Android apps are available in addition to the web interface.
faqs.question: Does Mastodon have mobile apps?

signserver.name: SignServer
signserver.short_description: Open-source, server-side signing software for code, documents, and timestamps, with a paid Enterprise edition.

features.description: Signing operations run server-side so cryptographic keys stay in one secured location instead of on individual machines.
features.title: Centralized key management

features.description: Supports signing in formats including JAR, CMS, OpenPGP, and Debian packages.
features.title: Multi-format code signing

features.description: Provides timestamping to prove when a document or artifact was signed.
features.title: Timestamping services

features.description: Supports signing for secure boot images and over-the-air firmware updates.
features.title: Secure boot and OTA signing

features.description: Includes support for post-quantum cryptographic algorithms.
features.title: Post-quantum cryptography support

features.description: Deployable via Docker, Kubernetes Helm charts, and Ansible for CI/CD signing workflows.
features.title: Container and DevOps integration

pros: Community Edition is free and licensed under LGPL v2.1
Supports a wide range of signing formats and use cases (code, documents, timestamps, firmware)
Enterprise edition adds SLAs and SaaS/Cloud/Appliance deployment options
Backed by Keyfactor, with Docker, Kubernetes, and Ansible deployment support
cons: Keyfactor states the Community Edition is not intended for production signing environments
Enterprise pricing is not publicly listed and requires a trial or sales contact
Requires technical setup (Docker/Kubernetes) rather than a hosted turnkey product

faqs.answer: The Community Edition is free and open source under LGPL v2.1. A paid Enterprise Edition with SLAs and additional security features is also available.
faqs.question: Is SignServer free?

faqs.answer: Keyfactor states the Community Edition is intended for learning, testing, and prototyping, not production signing environments; production use is recommended on the Enterprise Edition.
faqs.question: Can I use SignServer Community in production?

faqs.answer: SignServer can sign code (JAR, CMS, OpenPGP, Debian packages), documents, timestamps, and secure boot/OTA firmware images.
faqs.question: What can SignServer sign?

faqs.answer: It can be deployed via Docker, Kubernetes Helm charts, Ansible, or on cloud platforms like AWS and Azure.
faqs.question: How is SignServer deployed?

pricing_plans.price: Contact sales (30-day trial available on AWS/Azure)

paperless-ngx.name: Paperless-ngx
paperless-ngx.short_description: Free, open-source document management system that scans, OCRs, indexes, and archives paperwork for self-hosting.

features.description: Scanned documents are automatically processed with optical character recognition.
features.title: OCR ingestion

features.description: Search across the text content of all archived documents.
features.title: Full-text search

features.description: Documents can be tagged and organized for retrieval.
features.title: Tagging and organization

features.description: Deploys via Docker containers, with a pre-configured Docker Compose setup as the easiest path.
features.title: Docker Compose deployment

features.description: An Angular-based web UI provides access to the archive.
features.title: Web-based interface

pros: Completely free and open source under GPL-3.0
Large, active GitHub community (43.2k stars, 2.9k forks, 149 releases as of the latest check)
Straightforward Docker Compose deployment
Strong OCR and full-text search capabilities
cons: Documents are stored unencrypted; the project warns against running it on an untrusted host
Self-hosting requires ongoing technical maintenance
No official managed/hosted cloud version

faqs.answer: Yes, Paperless-ngx is fully free and open source under the GPL-3.0 license.
faqs.question: Is Paperless-ngx free?

faqs.answer: The recommended method is Docker Compose, using the project's pre-configured environment.
faqs.question: How do I deploy Paperless-ngx?

faqs.answer: The developers advise against running it on an untrusted host since documents are stored unencrypted; local, backed-up deployments are recommended for sensitive records.
faqs.question: Is Paperless-ngx safe for sensitive documents?

faqs.answer: The backend is built in Python (Django) and the frontend uses TypeScript with Angular.
faqs.question: What technology powers Paperless-ngx?
